skill.name: upgrading-expo
skill.description: Guides Expo projects through SDK upgrades, dependency alignment, configuration updates, and post-upgrade testing.
skill.summary: Plan and execute Expo SDK upgrades with compatibility checks and testing guidance.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged shell commands, GitHub URLs, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns. Review found legitimate Expo upgrade command examples and source links, while the crypto and reconnaissance findings are false positives caused by ordinary Expo SDK text. The skill is publishable with a warning because it instructs users to run package manager commands that modify projects and can fetch packages.

medium_findings.title: Project-Modifying Package Manager Commands
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The guide tells users to run Expo and npm commands that install or update packages. This is expected for an Expo upgrade skill, but these commands can modify dependencies, use the network, and affect local development environments.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command examples are explicit and directly related to the stated Expo upgrade purpose. Confidence is high that this is legitimate tooling guidance, with residual risk from package installation side effects.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded GitHub Source Links
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The hardcoded URLs point to the public Expo skill source repository. They do not transmit user data or request credentials.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Both URLs are visible documentation links to GitHub. No evidence found of hidden requests, tracking parameters, or exfiltration behavior.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Scanner False Positive
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: Static analysis flagged weak cryptography at Expo SDK text locations. No cryptographic algorithm, hashing function, encryption code, or security primitive appears at these lines.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines discuss Expo SDK upgrades and migration guides. No evidence found of MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or any cryptographic implementation.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Scanner False Positive
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The flagged line advises testing on iOS and Android. This is normal mobile quality assurance guidance, not host reconnaissance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is part of a testing checklist for mobile platforms. No evidence found of commands that enumerate host users, files, processes, network interfaces, or environment details.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Package Installation Commands
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes package installation and upgrade commands. Users should review package changes, use a feature branch, and avoid running commands in sensitive repositories without approval.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are present in shell code blocks and are expected for Expo upgrades. They are not malicious, but they perform network-backed dependency changes.

content.value_statement: Expo SDK upgrades often break dependencies, configuration, or native builds. This skill gives a structured upgrade path with checks before, during, and after the migration.
actual_capabilities: Plans Expo SDK upgrades with release notes, breaking changes, and package compatibility checks.
Recommends Expo CLI and expo install commands for SDK and dependency alignment.
Uses expo-doctor checks to identify incompatible or outdated packages.
Guides app.json and native configuration updates required by new SDK versions.
Defines post-upgrade testing across core app features, iOS, Android, and store builds.
limitations: Does not automate code changes or dependency installation by itself.
Does not include version-specific migration details for every Expo SDK release.
Requires users to review release notes and validate third-party package compatibility.
Cannot guarantee that native modules or app store builds will pass after an upgrade.

use_cases.title: Upgrade a Production Expo App
use_cases.description: Plan a controlled SDK migration with a feature branch, dependency checks, and focused regression testing.

use_cases.title: Resolve Expo Dependency Drift
use_cases.description: Use Expo tooling to align packages with the target SDK and identify compatibility warnings.

use_cases.title: Prepare for New Expo Features
use_cases.description: Move an app to a newer SDK so the team can adopt current Expo capabilities and tooling.

prompt_templates.title: Create an Upgrade Checklist
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the upgrading-expo skill to create a step-by-step checklist for upgrading my Expo app to the latest supported SDK.

prompt_templates.title: Review Breaking Changes
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the upgrading-expo skill to identify the main breaking-change areas I should inspect before upgrading this Expo project.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Dependency Alignment
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the upgrading-expo skill to plan how I should update Expo packages and verify dependency compatibility after the SDK upgrade.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Migration Test Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the upgrading-expo skill to create a release-focused test plan for an Expo SDK upgrade, including iOS, Android, runtime errors, and store builds.

output_examples.input: I need to upgrade an Expo app but want to reduce release risk.
output: A feature-branch upgrade plan with release note review first.
A dependency update sequence using Expo-compatible package checks.
A regression checklist covering app flows, native modules, and store builds.

output_examples.input: My Expo upgrade produced dependency warnings.
output: A troubleshooting path that prioritizes Expo package alignment.
Guidance to run compatibility checks after package updates.
A reminder to resolve peer dependency warnings before merging.

output_examples.input: I want to migrate deprecated Expo APIs after an SDK upgrade.
output: A review plan for migration guides and affected API calls.
A configuration update checklist for app.json and native files.
A focused testing list for changed features.

best_practices: Upgrade in a feature branch and keep a clean rollback path.
Review Expo release notes and migration guides before changing dependencies.
Run compatibility checks and test both iOS and Android before merging.
anti_patterns: Running upgrade commands directly on the main branch without review.
Ignoring peer dependency warnings after the SDK update.
Skipping app store build validation because local testing passed.

faq.question: Does this skill run the Expo upgrade automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides upgrade guidance and command suggestions that the user must review and run intentionally.

faq.question: Is this skill specific to one Expo SDK version?
faq.answer: No. It gives a general upgrade process that users should pair with the release notes for their target SDK.

faq.question: Why does the audit mark medium risk?
faq.answer: The skill includes package manager commands that can change dependencies and use the network. The commands appear legitimate for Expo upgrades.

faq.question: Can it help with deprecated APIs?
faq.answer: Yes. It prompts users to review migration guides, update deprecated API calls, and test affected features.

faq.question: Does it cover native build validation?
faq.answer: Yes. It reminds users to test native modules and verify app store builds after upgrading.

faq.question: What should users do before running upgrade commands?
faq.answer: They should create a feature branch, review release notes, and check third-party package compatibility.
